top_winning_behaviors.insight: Successful calls almost always establish concrete follow-up actions rather than leaving things vague.
top_winning_behaviors.booked_rate: 47 of 50 booked calls had clear next steps
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 94% of booked calls vs 55% of not booked calls

top_winning_behaviors.insight: The best calls flow smoothly without needing to overcome significant resistance from prospects.
top_winning_behaviors.booked_rate: 25 of 50 booked calls had no objections
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 50% of booked calls had zero objections vs 33% of not booked calls

top_winning_behaviors.insight: Calling prospects who already expressed interest dramatically increases conversion rates.
top_winning_behaviors.booked_rate: 29 of 48 demo request followup calls booked
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 60% conversion rate vs 19% for other opening angles

top_objections.best_response: Acknowledge the concern but redirect to value demonstration in the demo
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Still managed to book 3+ demos despite pricing objections

top_objections.frequency: Most common in not booked calls
top_objections.best_response: Probe deeper to understand specific reasons rather than accepting surface-level dismissal
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Successful reps rarely encounter this objection, suggesting better qualification

top_objections.best_response: Offer flexible scheduling options and emphasize brief time commitment
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Frame as working around their schedule rather than demanding specific times

opening_angle_analysis.insight: Following up with prospects who already showed interest yields 60% conversion rate because intent is pre-established
opening_angle_analysis.worst_insight: Fee savings angle only converts 10% of the time, likely because it focuses on cost rather than value and attracts price-sensitive prospects

tenure_insight: Experience doesn't appear to be the primary differentiator - mid-tenure reps actually have the best conversion rate at 33% (21/64), compared to senior reps at 38% (19/50) and new reps at 29% (10/35). This suggests that factors like call technique, opening angles, and territory quality may matter more than years of experience. The relatively similar performance across tenure levels indicates that the fundamentals can be learned quickly with proper training.
duration_insight: Successful calls average 6.4 minutes compared to 3.9 minutes for unsuccessful ones, representing a 64% longer duration. This isn't about talking more - it's about having substantive conversations. The longer duration likely reflects prospects asking questions, engaging in discovery, and working through scheduling details. Rushed calls under 4 minutes don't allow enough time to build rapport, understand needs, or overcome minor hesitations that naturally arise when booking a demo.
three_things_we_didnt_know: Social proof backfires - only 36% of booked calls used social proof vs 78% of failed calls, suggesting it may create skepticism or comparison shopping
Discovery questions aren't always necessary - only 58% of successful calls asked discovery questions, indicating that pre-qualified leads may not need extensive fact-finding
Strong objection handling still works - 7 calls with strong objections still booked demos, proving that persistence and skill can overcome significant resistance
